content.question: What is the quantified causal contribution of the 6/8-year gimnázium entry point (specifically) to Hungary's observed 53-point PISA performance gap and school segregation, versus the combined effect of other selection points (kindergarten entry, primary school choice, post-16 tracking) and capacity-allocation dynamics?
content.reduction_path: Commission a causal-decomposition study using quasi-experimental methods (e.g., regression discontinuity around historical changes in gimnázium entry age or capacity) or a counterfactual policy-simulation model that isolates the effect of removing only the age-10/12 selection while holding other junctures constant. Alternatively, survey or interview research with school choosers and administrators to map the relative importance of each selection point in parental decision-making and school enrollment patterns.
